chemistry.scope_summary: The paper demonstrates the cycloaddition of 9,10-diphenylanthracene with oxygen to form endoperoxide as a model reaction for testing the fluorescent fluid photochemical microreactor. Additionally, p-thiocresol oxidation was shown using Eosin Y as photocatalyst and FITC as light-converting medium.

optimization_table.key_findings: 400 ppm LR305 with 23V applied voltage and 86.6 min residence time gave 70% conversion; helical channels with 12 turns showed better mixing than linear arrays or cylinders

figures.flow_path: Reactant A and Reactant B flow through helical coil reactor containing fluorescent fluid
figures.key_information: Luminophors used: LR305 (440 nm → 630 nm), Rh6G (440 nm → 570 nm), FITC (440 nm → 530 nm). Methylene Blue photochemistry with O2, ACN at room temperature. Fluorescent fluid enables reaction monitoring through optical detection.
figures.caption: Figure 1.   a) Schematic illustration of the microchannel structure in FFPM and the light conversion process from the fluorescence fluid to reaction  liquid. b) The cycloaddition of DPA to the endoperoxide was used as a model reaction. c) Spectra overlapping scheme of the LR305/MB which provides  enhanced photon flux needed for the reaction channel.

figures.trend: relatively flat with slight increase for 100ppm conditions

figures.x_axis: Various parameters (Applied voltage, Residence time, Dye concentration)
figures.trend: Multiple optimization studies showing conversion vs different parameters

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Limited photon flux and wavelength mismatch between light source and photocatalyst absorption
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Fluorescent fluid microreactor enables wavelength conversion and enhanced photon flux through light-converting medium (LR305) that absorbs 440 nm blue LED light and emits at 630 nm matching methylene blue absorption
translation_logic.what_changed: Implemented 3D-printed microreactor with fluorescent fluid containing LR305 dye for wavelength conversion, enabling efficient photocatalytic oxidation
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Core photocatalytic oxidation chemistry using methylene blue as photosensitizer and oxygen as oxidant
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Enclosed microreactor system provides better control and safety compared to open batch systems

figure_descriptions: Figure 1: Reactor scheme showing microchannel structure and light conversion process
Figures 2-4: Optimization plots showing effects of various parameters on conversion

notes: Flow rate derived from Figure 3 caption showing 50 μL/min. No batch baseline provided as this work focuses on demonstrating the novel fluorescent fluid microreactor concept rather than batch-to-flow translation. High confidence in reactor design and optimization data from figures.
